description:
You are a data-scientist who is about to run a very memory-intensive data-cleaning pipeline.  
Before you start, you want to capture a concise snapshot of the current system status so that, if anything goes wrong, you can compare “before” and “after” values.

1. Create the directory  
   /home/user/diagnostics  
   if it does not already exist.

2. Inside that directory create a **plain-text** file named  
   /home/user/diagnostics/sys_snapshot.log

3. The file must consist of exactly **four sections**, one after the other, in the order shown below.  
   • Each section starts with a *section-header* line written **verbatim** (case-sensitive) and containing no extra characters or whitespace.  
   • Immediately after every header line, write the command output that the header describes (do **not** write the command itself).  
   • Insert one completely empty line **between** sections and **no** empty line at the very end of the file.

The required layout is therefore:

=== DATE & TIME ===  
<output of:  date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S'>  

=== DISK USAGE (/home/user) ===  
<output of:  df -h /home/user>  

=== MEMORY STATUS (MiB) ===  
<output of:  free -m>  

=== TOP 5 MEMORY PROCESSES ===  
<output of:  ps -eo pid,comm,%cpu,%mem --sort=-%mem | head -n 6>

4. Exact formatting rules that the automated checker will look for:

   a. The four header lines must appear **exactly** as shown (including spaces and upper-case letters).  
   b. Each header line must be followed by **at least one** data line produced by the indicated command.  
   c. After the final data line of the “TOP 5 MEMORY PROCESSES” section there must be **no trailing blank line**.  
   d. The “TOP 5 MEMORY PROCESSES” section must include the standard ps column header line that starts with “PID”.

5. When everything is done, you should have one file located at  
   /home/user/diagnostics/sys_snapshot.log  
   whose contents meet all of the formatting requirements above.

The deliverable for this task is solely the correctly-formatted sys_snapshot.log file; no additional output is required.
truth:
Expected artefacts that the grading script will verify:

1. Directory: /home/user/diagnostics  
   • Must exist and be writable by the regular user.

2. File: /home/user/diagnostics/sys_snapshot.log  
   • Must exist.  
   • Must contain exactly four header lines in this exact order:
     1) "=== DATE & TIME ==="
     2) "=== DISK USAGE (/home/user) ==="
     3) "=== MEMORY STATUS (MiB) ==="
     4) "=== TOP 5 MEMORY PROCESSES ==="
   • There must be one completely blank line between consecutive sections.  
   • There must be no blank line after the final data line.  
   • The “TOP 5 MEMORY PROCESSES” section must include a line that begins with “PID ” (including a space after PID).  
   • The log file must not contain the literal commands, only their output.

The grader will allow the actual numeric values inside the data sections to vary; it will only check the file path, header lines, blank-line placement, presence of at least one data line per section, and the inclusion of the “PID” header inside the final section.
name: task_000000_be2a64e9
